## Authentication Service

### auth.service.ts

```typescript
/**
 * Authentication service handling user login, registration, and token management
 */
export class AuthService {
  constructor(
    private readonly usersService: UsersService,
    private readonly jwtService: JwtService,
    private readonly configService: ConfigService,
    private readonly cacheManager: Cache,
    private readonly eventEmitter: EventEmitter2
  ) {}

  /**
   * Refreshes an authentication token
   * @param refreshToken The refresh token to validate
   * @returns New access and refresh tokens
   */
  async refreshToken(refreshToken: string): Promise<TokenResponse> {
    try {
      // Verify the refresh token
      const payload = await this.jwtService.verifyAsync(refreshToken, {
        secret: this.configService.get('JWT_REFRESH_SECRET')
      });
      
      // Check if token is in blacklist
      const isBlacklisted = await this.cacheManager.get(`blacklist:${refreshToken}`);
      if (isBlacklisted) {
        throw new UnauthorizedException('Refresh token is invalid');
      }
      
      // Generate new tokens
      const user = await this.usersService.findOne(payload.sub);
      const tokens = await this.generateTokens(user);
      
      // Blacklist the old refresh token
      await this.cacheManager.set(
        `blacklist:${refreshToken}`,
        true,
        { ttl: this.configService.get('JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION') }
      );
      
      // Emit refresh token event
      this.eventEmitter.emit('auth.token.refreshed', {
        userId: user.id,
        timestamp: new Date()
      });
      
      return tokens;
    } catch (error) {
      throw new UnauthorizedException('Failed to refresh token');
    }
  }
}
```

### auth.controller.ts

```typescript
@Controller('auth')
export class AuthController {
  constructor(private readonly authService: AuthService) {}

  /**
   * Refresh access token using a valid refresh token
   */
  @Post('refresh')
  async refreshToken(@Body() refreshTokenDto: RefreshTokenDto): Promise<TokenResponse> {
    return this.authService.refreshToken(refreshTokenDto.refreshToken);
  }
}
```

## Notification System

### notification.service.ts

```typescript
/**
 * Service for managing user notifications
 */
export class NotificationService {
  constructor(
    @InjectRepository(Notification)
    private notificationRepository: Repository<Notification>,
    private readonly eventEmitter: EventEmitter2,
    private readonly userService: UserService,
    private readonly emailService: EmailService,
    private readonly configService: ConfigService,
    private readonly logger: Logger
  ) {}

  /**
   * Gets user notification preferences
   * @param userId The user ID
   * @returns User notification preferences
   */
  async getUserPreferences(userId: string): Promise<NotificationPreferences> {
    const user = await this.userService.findOne(userId);
    
    if (!user) {
      throw new NotFoundException('User not found');
    }
    
    return user.notificationPreferences || this.getDefaultPreferences();
  }
  
  /**
   * Updates user notification preferences
   * @param userId The user ID
   * @param preferences New notification preferences
   * @returns Updated notification preferences
   */
  async updateUserPreferences(
    userId: string, 
    preferences: UpdateNotificationPreferencesDto
  ): Promise<NotificationPreferences> {
    const user = await this.userService.findOne(userId);
    
    if (!user) {
      throw new NotFoundException('User not found');
    }
    
    const currentPreferences = user.notificationPreferences || this.getDefaultPreferences();
    const updatedPreferences = {
      ...currentPreferences,
      ...preferences
    };
    
    await this.userService.update(userId, {
      notificationPreferences: updatedPreferences
    });
    
    // Emit preference updated event
    this.eventEmitter.emit('notification.preferences.updated', {
      userId,
      preferences: updatedPreferences,
      timestamp: new Date()
    });
    
    this.logger.log(`Updated notification preferences for user ${userId}`);
    
    return updatedPreferences;
  }
  
  /**
   * Gets default notification preferences
   */
  private getDefaultPreferences(): NotificationPreferences {
    return {
      email: {
        marketing: false,
        security: true,
        social: true,
        updates: true
      },
      push: {
        marketing: false,
        security: true,
        social: true,
        updates: false
      },
      frequency: NotificationFrequency.IMMEDIATE
    };
  }
}
```

## Admin Dashboard

### admin-analytics.component.ts

```typescript
@Component({
  selector: 'app-admin-analytics',
  templateUrl: './admin-analytics.component.html',
  styleUrls: ['./admin-analytics.component.scss']
})
export class AdminAnalyticsComponent implements OnInit, OnDestroy {
  public userMetrics: UserMetrics;
  public contentMetrics: ContentMetrics;
  public timeRange = '7d';
  public isLoading = true;
  public error: string | null = null;
  
  private destroy$ = new Subject<void>();
  
  constructor(
    private analyticsService: AnalyticsService,
    private authService: AuthService
  ) {}
  
  ngOnInit(): void {
    this.loadAnalytics();
  }
  
  ngOnDestroy(): void {
    this.destroy$.next();
    this.destroy$.complete();
  }
  
  /**
   * Loads analytics data based on the selected time range
   */
  loadAnalytics(): void {
    this.isLoading = true;
    this.error = null;
    
    this.analyticsService.getAdminMetrics(this.timeRange)
      .pipe(
        takeUntil(this.destroy$),
        finalize(() => {
          this.isLoading = false;
        })
      )
      .subscribe({
        next: (metrics) => {
          this.userMetrics = metrics.userMetrics;
          this.contentMetrics = metrics.contentMetrics;
        },
        error: (err) => {
          this.error = 'Failed to load analytics data. Please try again.';
          console.error('Analytics error:', err);
        }
      });
  }
  
  /**
   * Changes the time range and reloads data
   */
  changeTimeRange(range: string): void {
    this.timeRange = range;
    this.loadAnalytics();
  }
  
  /**
   * Exports analytics data as CSV
   */
  exportData(): void {
    this.analyticsService.exportMetrics(this.timeRange)
      .pipe(takeUntil(this.destroy$))
      .subscribe({
        next: (blob) => {
          const url = window.URL.createObjectURL(blob);
          const a = document.createElement('a');
          a.href = url;
          a.download = `analytics-${this.timeRange}-${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]}.csv`;
          document.body.appendChild(a);
          a.click();
          window.URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
          a.remove();
        },
        error: (err) => {
          this.error = 'Failed to export data. Please try again.';
          console.error('Export error:', err);
        }
      });
  }
}
```

## Test Improvements

### auth.service.spec.ts

```typescript
describe('AuthService', () => {
  let service: AuthService;
  let usersService: MockUsersService;
  let jwtService: MockJwtService;
  let configService: MockConfigService;
  let cacheManager: MockCacheManager;
  let eventEmitter: MockEventEmitter;

  beforeEach(async () => {
    const module: TestingModule = await Test.createTestingModule({
      providers: [
        AuthService,
        {
          provide: UsersService,
          useClass: MockUsersService,
        },
        {
          provide: JwtService,
          useClass: MockJwtService,
        },
        {
          provide: ConfigService,
          useClass: MockConfigService,
        },
        {
          provide: CACHE_MANAGER,
          useClass: MockCacheManager,
        },
        {
          provide: EventEmitter2,
          useClass: MockEventEmitter,
        },
      ],
    }).compile();

    service = module.get<AuthService>(AuthService);
    usersService = module.get(UsersService);
    jwtService = module.get(JwtService);
    configService = module.get(ConfigService);
    cacheManager = module.get(CACHE_MANAGER);
    eventEmitter = module.get(EventEmitter2);
  });

  it('should be defined', () => {
    expect(service).toBeDefined();
  });

  describe('refreshToken', () => {
    it('should refresh tokens when valid refresh token is provided', async () => {
      // Arrange
      const refreshToken = 'valid.refresh.token';
      const userId = 'user-123';
      const user = { id: userId, email: 'test@example.com' };
      const newTokens = {
        accessToken: 'new.access.token',
        refreshToken: 'new.refresh.token',
        expiresIn: 3600,
      };
      
      jwtService.verifyAsync.mockResolvedValue({ sub: userId });
      cacheManager.get.mockResolvedValue(null);
      usersService.findOne.mockResolvedValue(user);
      
      // Mock generateTokens using spyOn since it's a method of the service being tested
      jest.spyOn(service, 'generateTokens').mockResolvedValue(newTokens);
      
      // Act
      const result = await service.refreshToken(refreshToken);
      
      // Assert
      expect(result).toEqual(newTokens);
      expect(jwtService.verifyAsync).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        refreshToken,
        { secret: configService.get('JWT_REFRESH_SECRET') }
      );
      expect(cacheManager.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith(`blacklist:${refreshToken}`);
      expect(usersService.findOne).toHaveBeenCalledWith(userId);
      expect(service.generateTokens).toHaveBeenCalledWith(user);
      expect(cacheManager.set).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        `blacklist:${refreshToken}`,
        true,
        { ttl: configService.get('JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRATION') }
      );
      expect(eventEmitter.emit).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        'auth.token.refreshed',
        expect.objectContaining({
          userId,
          timestamp: expect.any(Date)
        })
      );
    });

    it('should throw UnauthorizedException when refresh token is blacklisted', async () => {
      // Arrange
      const refreshToken = 'blacklisted.token';
      const userId = 'user-123';
      
      jwtService.verifyAsync.mockResolvedValue({ sub: userId });
      cacheManager.get.mockResolvedValue(true);
      
      // Act & Assert
      await expect(service.refreshToken(refreshToken)).rejects.toThrow(
        new UnauthorizedException('Refresh token is invalid')
      );
      expect(jwtService.verifyAsync).toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(cacheManager.get).toHaveBeenCalledWith(`blacklist:${refreshToken}`);
      expect(usersService.findOne).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    it('should throw UnauthorizedException when JWT verification fails', async () => {
      // Arrange
      const refreshToken = 'invalid.token';
      jwtService.verifyAsync.mockRejectedValue(new Error('Invalid token'));
      
      // Act & Assert
      await expect(service.refreshToken(refreshToken)).rejects.toThrow(
        new UnauthorizedException('Failed to refresh token')
      );
      expect(jwtService.verifyAsync).toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(cacheManager.get).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(usersService.findOne).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });
  });
});
```

## Performance Improvements

### query-optimization.ts

```typescript
/**
 * Optimized query for retrieving user notifications with pagination
 */
export async function getUserNotifications(
  userId: string,
  page: number = 1,
  limit: number = 20,
  entityManager: EntityManager
): Promise<{ notifications: Notification[]; total: number }> {
  // Use query builder for efficient pagination
  const queryBuilder = entityManager
    .createQueryBuilder(Notification, 'notification')
    .leftJoinAndSelect('notification.sender', 'sender')
    .where('notification.recipientId = :userId', { userId })
    .orderBy('notification.createdAt', 'DESC')
    .skip((page - 1) * limit)
    .take(limit);
  
  // Selectively load only the fields we need
  queryBuilder.select([
    'notification.id',
    'notification.type',
    'notification.content',
    'notification.isRead',
    'notification.createdAt',
    'sender.id',
    'sender.username',
    'sender.avatarUrl'
  ]);
  
  // Execute count query separately with optimized count
  const total = await entityManager
    .createQueryBuilder(Notification, 'notification')
    .where('notification.recipientId = :userId', { userId })
    .getCount();
  
  // Add caching for frequent reads
  queryBuilder.cache(true, 60 * 1000); // 1 minute cache
  
  const notifications = await queryBuilder.getMany();
  
  return {
    notifications,
    total
  };
}
```

## Bug Fixes

### email-templating.service.ts

```typescript
/**
 * Fixed issue with email template rendering for special characters
 */
export class EmailTemplatingService {
  // ... existing code

  /**
   * Renders an email template with provided data
   * @param templateName The template name to render
   * @param data Template variables
   * @returns Rendered HTML content
   */
  async renderTemplate(templateName: string, data: Record<string, any>): Promise<string> {
    try {
      // Fix: Proper handling of special characters
      const sanitizedData = this.sanitizeTemplateData(data);
      
      const template = await this.loadTemplate(templateName);
      if (!template) {
        throw new NotFoundException(`Template ${templateName} not found`);
      }
      
      // Fix: Added proper error handling for template rendering
      const rendered = await this.handlebars.render(template, sanitizedData);
      return rendered;
    } catch (error) {
      this.logger.error(`Failed to render template ${templateName}`, error.stack);
      throw new InternalServerErrorException('Failed to render email template');
    }
  }
  
  /**
   * Sanitizes template data to prevent XSS and handle special characters
   * @param data The data to sanitize
   * @returns Sanitized data
   */
  private sanitizeTemplateData(data: Record<string, any>): Record<string, any> {
    // Fix: Added proper sanitization for HTML content
    const sanitized = { ...data };
    
    Object.keys(sanitized).forEach(key => {
      if (typeof sanitized[key] === 'string') {
        // Only sanitize non-HTML content fields
        if (!key.includes('Html') && !key.includes('html')) {
          sanitized[key] = this.escapeHtml(sanitized[key]);
        }
      } else if (sanitized[key] !== null && typeof sanitized[key] === 'object') {
        sanitized[key] = this.sanitizeTemplateData(sanitized[key]);
      }
    });
    
    return sanitized;
  }
  
  /**
   * Escapes HTML special characters
   * @param text Text to escape
   * @returns Escaped text
   */
  private escapeHtml(text: string): string {
    return text
      .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
      .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
      .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
      .replace(/"/g, '&quot;')
      .replace(/'/g, '&#039;');
  }
}
```

## Summary

Today's changes focused on three key areas:

1. **Authentication Enhancements**
   - Implemented JWT token refresh functionality
   - Added security improvements for token blacklisting
   - Created comprehensive unit tests for authentication flows

2. **Notification System Progress**
   - Added user notification preferences management
   - Optimized notification queries for better performance
   - Fixed bug in email template rendering for special characters

3. **Admin Dashboard Development**
   - Created analytics component for the admin dashboard
   - Implemented data export functionality
   - Added interactive time range selection for metrics
